metadata.scope: Multi-session Claude Code orchestration — sandbox, agents, requirements BCs
metadata.goal: Enable two simultaneous Claude Code sessions orchestrated through a single requirement

domain_events.name: SandboxCreated
domain_events.trigger: Sandbox.create(workspace_id, project_id, config)

integration.channel: event store — awaiting consumer in agents BC

domain_events.name: SandboxStarted
domain_events.trigger: Sandbox.start()

integration.channel: event store — awaiting consumer in agents BC

domain_events.notes: STARTING intermediate state collapsed for MVP

domain_events.name: SandboxExecutionCompleted
domain_events.trigger: Sandbox.execute(exit_code, duration, stdout, stderr)

integration.channel: event store — CRITICAL: must be consumed by agents BC for work item completion

domain_events.notes: Key bridge event between sandbox and agents BCs. Currently published but never consumed.

domain_events.name: SandboxStopped
domain_events.trigger: Sandbox.stop()
domain_events.notes: STOPPING intermediate state collapsed for MVP

domain_events.name: SandboxDestroyed
domain_events.trigger: Sandbox.destroy()

integration.channel: event store — should trigger worktree cleanup

domain_events.notes: Terminal event. SubprocessSandboxBackend.destroy() does best-effort worktree cleanup.

domain_events.name: SandboxFailed
domain_events.trigger: Sandbox.fail(reason, detail)

integration.channel: event store — CRITICAL: must be consumed by agents BC for work item failure

domain_events.notes: Terminal event. Reason enum: timeout|resource_limit|error.

domain_events.name: AgentSpawned
domain_events.trigger: Agent.spawn(name, description, capabilities)

domain_events.name: AgentAssigned
domain_events.trigger: Agent.assign(work_item_id)

domain_events.name: AgentExecutionStarted
domain_events.trigger: Agent.start_execution()

domain_events.name: AgentExecutionCompleted
domain_events.trigger: Agent.complete_execution()

domain_events.name: AgentFailed
domain_events.trigger: Agent.fail(reason, detail)
domain_events.notes: Terminal event.

domain_events.name: AgentTerminated
domain_events.trigger: Agent.terminate(reason)
domain_events.notes: Terminal event. Graceful shutdown.

domain_events.name: WorkItemCreated
domain_events.trigger: WorkItem.create(milestone_id, requirement_id, project_id, required_capabilities, acceptance_criteria)

domain_events.name: WorkItemAssigned
domain_events.trigger: WorkItem.assign(agent_id)

domain_events.name: WorkItemStarted
domain_events.trigger: WorkItem.start()

domain_events.name: WorkItemCompleted
domain_events.trigger: WorkItem.complete(result_summary, config_hash)

integration.channel: consumed by process manager for progress tracking

domain_events.notes: Terminal event.

domain_events.name: WorkItemFailed
domain_events.trigger: WorkItem.fail(reason, config_hash)

integration.channel: consumed by process manager for progress tracking

domain_events.notes: Terminal event.

domain_events.name: WorkItemReassigned
domain_events.trigger: WorkItem.reassign(new_agent_id, reason)
domain_events.notes: Clears collaborators on reassignment.

domain_events.name: CollaboratorJoined
domain_events.trigger: WorkItem.join_collaborator(collaborator_agent_id, capability, step_name)
domain_events.notes: Max 3 collaborators per work item.

domain_events.name: CollaboratorCompleted
domain_events.trigger: WorkItem.complete_collaboration(collaborator_agent_id, step_name, result_summary)

domain_events.name: RequirementDeclared
domain_events.trigger: Requirement.declare(project_id, description, goals, constraints)

domain_events.name: RequirementDecomposed
domain_events.trigger: Requirement.decompose(milestones)

payload.type: list[dict] with title, description, acceptance_criteria, estimated_effort, dependencies, required_capabilities

integration.channel: process manager via direct method call (handle_requirement_decomposed)

domain_events.notes: Primary cross-BC integration event. Triggers work item creation and agent assignment.

domain_events.name: RequirementStarted
domain_events.trigger: Requirement.start()
domain_events.notes: EXISTS but UNUSED. No code path currently calls Requirement.start(). Needed for Epic 4.

domain_events.name: RequirementCompleted
domain_events.trigger: Requirement.complete()
domain_events.notes: EXISTS but UNUSED. Should be triggered by process manager when all work items complete. Terminal.

domain_events.name: RequirementFailed
domain_events.trigger: Requirement.fail(failure_reason)
domain_events.notes: EXISTS but UNUSED. Terminal event.

domain_events.name: WorkItemSandboxProvisioned
domain_events.trigger: Executor provisions sandbox + worktree before starting work item execution

integration.channel: links work item to its sandbox — enables cleanup and observability

domain_events.notes: MISSING. Epic 1 needs this to track which sandbox/worktree is assigned to which work item. Currently no linkage exists between these aggregates.

domain_events.name: ClaudeCodeSessionInvoked
domain_events.trigger: SubprocessSandboxBackend.execute() with Claude Code CLI command

integration.channel: sandbox BC internal — subprocess lifecycle tracking

domain_events.notes: MISSING. Epic 2 needs this to track Claude Code session lifecycle. Currently SubprocessSandboxBackend runs arbitrary scripts with no typed invocation event.

domain_events.name: ConcurrentDispatchStarted
domain_events.trigger: Process manager dispatches multiple work items simultaneously via asyncio.gather
domain_events.notes: MISSING. Epic 3 needs this to mark transition from sequential to concurrent dispatch. Currently execute_requirement() uses sequential for-loop.

domain_events.name: WorkItemExecutionQueued
domain_events.trigger: Concurrent dispatcher queues work item for execution
domain_events.notes: MISSING. Epic 3 needs this for observability into concurrent task scheduling. Enables tracking queue depth and wait times.

domain_events.name: SandboxExecutionEnvironmentReady
domain_events.trigger: SubprocessSandboxBackend signals that sandbox and worktree are ready for execution
domain_events.notes: MISSING. Epic 1 needs this as a readiness signal. Currently no handshake exists between sandbox provisioning and agent execution start.

boundary_assumptions.statement: The Sandbox aggregate owns subprocess lifecycle (create, start, execute, stop, destroy) and the WorkItem aggregate does NOT directly manage subprocesses.
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: team discussion — should the executor or the sandbox BC own the subprocess lifecycle?

boundary_assumptions.statement: SandboxExecutionCompleted carries exit_code, stdout, stderr, and duration_seconds — this is sufficient for agents BC to determine work item success or failure without additional data.
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: Epic 2 implementation — Claude Code output may require structured parsing beyond raw stdout

boundary_assumptions.statement: SandboxCreated -> SandboxStarted -> SandboxExecutionCompleted always precedes WorkItemCompleted. The sandbox lifecycle must complete before the work item can transition to terminal state.
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: Epic 1 integration — need to verify this ordering holds under concurrent dispatch

boundary_assumptions.statement: The process manager (RequirementExecutionProcessManager) is the sole coordinator for cross-BC orchestration. It owns the decision of when to create sandboxes, which agents to assign, and when to dispatch concurrently.
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: architecture review — DD-002 says flat pool, but process manager may need more authority for concurrent dispatch

boundary_assumptions.statement: The Claude Code CLI invocation uses --dangerously-skip-permissions and --print flags, passes the task prompt via stdin or --prompt flag, and returns structured output on stdout that can be parsed into an ExecutionResult.
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: Epic 2 spike — need to verify Claude Code CLI interface and output format

boundary_assumptions.statement: WorktreeManager is owned by the sandbox BC, not a shared utility. Each sandbox gets its own worktree, and the worktree lifecycle is tied to the sandbox lifecycle.
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: already confirmed in codebase — WorktreeManager lives in sandbox package

boundary_assumptions.statement: Under concurrent dispatch, multiple SandboxCreated events can occur simultaneously, but each sandbox operates independently. There is no global ordering constraint between concurrent sandbox lifecycles.
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: Epic 3 — concurrent execution may reveal ordering dependencies not visible in sequential flow

boundary_assumptions.statement: The event store subscription mechanism (InMemoryEventStore.subscribe()) should be the primary channel for cross-BC event propagation, replacing the current direct method calls from API layer to process manager.
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: architecture review — current direct wiring works but doesn't scale to concurrent dispatch where events arrive asynchronously

metadata.scope: Collaborative storytelling (whole domain — session lifecycle, jam collaboration, workflow phases, client UI state)
metadata.goal: Improve collaborative storytelling using tried and tested approaches to product development

domain_events.name: SessionCreated
domain_events.trigger: User initiates Start a Session via POST /api/sessions with creatorName
domain_events.state_change: non-existent -> new session with code, creator participant, empty submissions

domain_events.name: ParticipantJoined
domain_events.trigger: User joins via join code POST /api/sessions/:code/join with participantName

domain_events.name: SubmissionReceived
domain_events.trigger: Participant submits YAML file via POST /api/sessions/:code/submit
domain_events.notes: Validates YAML against candidate-events.schema.json before accepting

domain_events.name: JamSessionStarted
domain_events.trigger: Facilitator calls POST /api/sessions/:code/jam/start or jam_start MCP tool
domain_events.state_change: jam null -> JamArtifacts with empty resolutions, ownership map, unresolved items
domain_events.notes: Idempotent — re-requesting returns existing jam rather than creating new one

domain_events.name: ConflictResolved
domain_events.trigger: Facilitator records conflict resolution via POST /api/sessions/:code/jam/resolve

domain_events.name: AggregateOwnershipAssigned
domain_events.trigger: Facilitator assigns aggregate ownership via POST /api/sessions/:code/jam/assign
domain_events.state_change: ownershipMap -> OwnershipAssignment added or replaced for aggregate

domain_events.name: UnresolvedItemFlagged
domain_events.trigger: Facilitator flags unresolved item via flagUnresolved on SessionStore
domain_events.notes: MCP interface not yet exposed in http.ts — only available via store directly

domain_events.name: ContractBundleLoaded
domain_events.trigger: System loads contract output from /formalize via MCP contract_load tool

domain_events.name: IntegrationReportLoaded
domain_events.trigger: System loads integration report via MCP integration_load tool
domain_events.notes: Final validation before workflow completion; determines if session reaches done phase

domain_events.name: SessionPersisted
domain_events.trigger: Any state mutation triggers persistence to data/sessions.json
domain_events.state_change: in-memory session -> serialized to JSON file

domain_events.name: WorkflowPhaseTransitioned
domain_events.trigger: Session state changes trigger workflow phase inference (computed from artifact inventory)
domain_events.state_change: previous workflow phase -> next workflow phase
domain_events.notes: Phases inferred not commanded: lobby -> prep -> compare -> jam -> formalize -> integrate -> done

domain_events.name: FileLoaded
domain_events.trigger: User loads a YAML file via file picker or drag-and-drop in the client UI
domain_events.state_change: files list -> LoadedFile added or replaced by role
domain_events.notes: Auto-switches to comparison view if 2+ files loaded

domain_events.name: FileRemoved
domain_events.trigger: User removes a loaded file from the client UI
domain_events.state_change: files list -> LoadedFile with matching role removed
domain_events.notes: Reverts view to cards if fewer than 2 files remain

domain_events.name: ViewModeChanged
domain_events.trigger: User switches between cards, flow, or comparison view
domain_events.state_change: activeView -> switched between cards, flow, or comparison

domain_events.name: FilterApplied
domain_events.trigger: User toggles confidence level or direction filter in the UI

domain_events.name: AggregateSelected
domain_events.trigger: User clicks an aggregate to filter or highlight related events
domain_events.state_change: selectedAggregate -> set to aggregate name or null

boundary_assumptions.statement: ContractBundleLoaded assumes /formalize produces EventContract[] and BoundaryContract[] with a specific shape defined in src/server/types.ts
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: Review /formalize output schema against ContractBundle type definition

boundary_assumptions.statement: IntegrationReportLoaded assumes /integrate produces checks with pass|fail|warn|skip status and an overall status field
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: Review /integrate output schema against IntegrationReport type definition

boundary_assumptions.statement: Workflow phases progress linearly: lobby -> prep -> compare -> jam -> formalize -> integrate -> done, with no backward transitions
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: Review workflow-engine.ts phase transition logic for backward transitions

boundary_assumptions.statement: YAML submissions validate against candidate-events.schema.json which is the shared contract with the tackline pipeline
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: Schema is checked into src/schema/candidate-events.schema.json

boundary_assumptions.statement: Client-side UI state events (FileLoaded, FileRemoved, ViewModeChanged, FilterApplied, AggregateSelected) belong to the AppState bounded context, separate from the Session server-side domain
boundary_assumptions.verify_with: Confirm whether client-side state should be modeled as a separate bounded context or folded into Session
